[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:last 150 pages as of feb qq]][[ja:積川神社]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] {{Shrine | Name=Tsugawa Shrine | image=[[File:Tsugawa-jinja1.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden and Second Torii{{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.446|frame-longitude=135.414|coord={{coord|34|25|30.42|N|135|26|21.75|E}}}} | location=[[Osaka Prefecture]][[Kishiwada City]] Tsugawa Town 350 | coordinates={{coord|34|25|30.42|N|135|26|21.75|E|scale:30000}} | deity=Ikui-no-Kami
Sakui-no-Kami
Tsunagai-no-Kami
Asuha-no-Kami
Hahiki-no-Kami | Shrine Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (small)
[[Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an [[Shikinai-sha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites * {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izumi Province]] [[Category:Fourth Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kishiwada City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February qq 2025]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]][[en:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:new pages]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] ]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:SuQqqq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Qqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:ekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[sn:ekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]]

Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an [[Shikinai-sha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites * {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Asuha-no-Kami
Hahiki-no-Kami | Shrine Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (small)
[[Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an [[Shikinai-sha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izumi Province]] [[Category:Fourth Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kishiwada City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] ]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:ekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:ekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]]

The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - It is an [[Shikinai-sha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - {{Shrine | Name=Tsugawa Shrine | image=[[File:Tsugawa-jinja1.jpg|250px|Haiden]]
Haiden and Second Torii{{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.446|frame-longitude=135.414|coord={{coord|34|25|30.42|N|135|26|21.75|E}}}} | location=[[Osaka Prefecture]][[Kishiwada City]] Tsugawa Town 350 | coordinates={{coord|34|25|30.42|N|135|26|21.75|E|scale:30000}} | deity=Ikui-no-Kami
Sakui-no-Kami
Tsunagai-no-Kami
Asuha-no-Kami
Hahiki-no-Kami | Shrine Rank=[[Shikinai-sha]] (small)
[[Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an [[Shikinai-sha]] and formerly held the rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} ]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Izumi Province]] [[Category:Fourth Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada City]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kishiwada City]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[en:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:ekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]]

In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=draft|12=draft}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=draft|12=draft}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an [[Shikinaisha]] and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - Haiden and Second Torii{{Maplink2|zoom=12|frame=yes|plain=yes|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|frame-height=220|type=point|frame-latitude=34.446|frame-longitude=135.414|coord={{coord|34|25|30.42|N|135|26|21.75|E}}}} | location=[[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[Kishiwada City]] Tsugawa Town 350 | coordinates={{coord|34|25|30.42|N|135|26|21.75|E|scale:30000}} | deity=Ikui-no-Kami

[[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:ekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}}

These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:ekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}}

Hahiki-no-Kami | Shrine Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
[[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|qid=Q740456}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[sn:Sekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[sn:ekigawa Shrine (Kishiwada)]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Hahiki-no-Kami | Shrine Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
[[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|qid=Q740456}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the [[Ikasuri no Kami|Zazami-no-Kami]]. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11597137}}

It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11597137}}

These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|WD=Q740456}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11597137}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11597137}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] {{wikidata link|Q1}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]][[ja:積川神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597137]] Wikidata name: Sekikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Tsugawa Shrine | image=File:Tsugawa-jinja1.jpg | location=[[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[Kishiwada City]] Tsugawa Town 350 | coordinates={{Coord|34.425117|N|135.439375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
[[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|WD=Q740456}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11597137}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11597137}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]][[ja:積川神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597137]] Wikidata name: Sekikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Tsugawa Shrine | image=File:Tsugawa-jinja1.jpg | location=[[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[Kishiwada City]] Tsugawa Town 350 | coordinates={{Coord|34.425117|N|135.439375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
[[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|WD=Q740456}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11597137}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11597137}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|WD=Q740456}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - [[commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]][[ja:積川神社]] [[commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]][[ja:積川神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]][[ja:積川神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597137]] Wikidata name: Sekikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Tsugawa Shrine | image=File:Tsugawa-jinja1.jpg | location=[[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[Kishiwada City]] Tsugawa Town 350 | coordinates={{Coord|34.425117|N|135.439375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
[[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|WD=Q740456}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]]{{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] }} [[ja:積川神社]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:積川神社]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. [[commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]][[ja:積川神社]] [[commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]][[ja:積川神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]][[ja:積川神社]] {{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11597137]] Wikidata name: Sekikawa Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Tsugawa Shrine | image=File:Tsugawa-jinja1.jpg | location=[[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]][[Kishiwada City]] Tsugawa Town 350 | coordinates={{Coord|34.425117|N|135.439375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
[[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|WD=Q740456}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:積川神社]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:積川神社]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|WD=Q740456}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]], Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of [[:en:Izumi Province|Izumi Province]] and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara no Michizane]] * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:積川神社]] {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province}} and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]].
