According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. { {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Tsugawa Shrine | image=File:Tsugawa-jinja1.jpg | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}[[Kishiwada City]] Tsugawa Town 350 | coordinates={{Coord|34.425117|N|135.439375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|WD=Q740456|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Izumi district|ja|和泉郡|zh|和泉郡|WD=Q10982247|1=Izumi district}} || {{ill|Tsugawa Shrine|lt=Tsugawa Shrine|ja|積川神社|commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja|WD=Q11597137|1=Tsugawa Shrine}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} ({{ill|Shrines receiving hoe offering|WD=Q135009205|lt=Hoe|1=Shrines receiving Hoe offering}}) || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || 6 seats, {{ill|Shi-no-Miya|WD=Q134917307|lt=Shi-no-Miya|1=Shi-no-Miya}} || — || {{coord|34.425060|135.439460|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181008 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11597137}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]] [[ja:積川神社]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shinomiya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed curly braces]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe offering]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

{ {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | Name=Tsugawa Shrine | image=File:Tsugawa-jinja1.jpg | location={{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}[[Kishiwada City]] Tsugawa Town 350 | coordinates={{Coord|34.425117|N|135.439375|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} (small)
{{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} Fourth Shrine
Former [[Gōsha]] | Founded=During the reign of Emperor Sujin | Main Hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri | Annual Festival=[[October 8]] | prefecture={{ill|Kishiwada|WD=Q740456|1=Kishiwada, Osaka}} }} '''Tsugawa Shrine''' (積川神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in Tsugawa Town, [[Kishiwada City]], {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}}, Japan. It is an {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|lt=Shikinaisha}} and formerly held the Rank of [[Gōsha]]. It is considered the Fourth Shrine of {{ill|Izumi Province|en|Izumi Province|WD=Q1047117|lt=Izumi Province|1=Izumi Province}} and was an imperial prayer site. == Deities == * Main Deities - Ikui-no-Kami, Sakui-no-Kami, Tsunagai-no-Kami, Asuha-no-Kami, Hahiki-no-Kami. These deities are collectively known as the {{ill|Ikasuri no Kami|WD=Q10928586|1=Ikasuri no Kami}}. == History == The shrine is said to have been established during the reign of Emperor Sujin and is recorded in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' compiled in 927. In 864, it was Ranked Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, and elevated to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper Grade in 873. According to shrine legend, in 1090, Emperor Shirakawa visited Kumano and wrote the shrine’s plaque himself, replacing an earlier one with poor calligraphy. The shrine received various imperial patronages and was linked to important historical events, including land grants and restorations ordered by figures like Toyotomi Hideyori in 1603. It became a [[Gōsha]] in 1873 and was designated an Important Cultural Property in 1950. == {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} == The Tsugawa Shrine {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=worship site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} is located along the [[Kumano Kodō|Kumano Kodo]] route in Komatsusato Town, allowing distant parishioners to worship. == Grounds == * Main Hall (Important Cultural Property) - Renovated in 1603 by Toyotomi Hideyori. * Stone Lanterns - Donated by Kusunoki Masanori. * Middle Gate * Worship Hall * Shrine Office * Tsugawa Town Shrine Hall * External Sites ** {{ill|Haisho|ja|拝所|lt=Worship Site|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haisho|qq=}} (Otabisho) === Subsidiary Shrines === * Tenjinsha - deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|en|Sugawara no Michizane|WD=Q382005|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * Yasaka Shrine - deity: [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto]] * Shiraga Shrine - deity: [[Sarutahiko Ōkami|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto]] * Tsugawa Ebisu Shrine - deity: [[Kotoshironushi-no-Kami]] == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Property === * Main Hall - Designated in 1914. === Osaka Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties === * Wooden Male and Female deity Statues - Kamakura Period. * Plaque - Inscribed by Emperor Shirakawa in 1090. === Kishiwada City Designated Natural Monuments === * Mukuro Tree === Others === * Portable Shrine - Believed to be dedicated by Yodogimi. * Ancient Mirror == Festivals == * Annual Festival: October 8 == References == * Tsugawa Shrine History == External Links == * [http://www.toric-inc.com/tsugawa/ Tsugawa Shrine] (Official Website) {{Commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja}} {{translated page|ja|積川神社|version=92836742|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsugawa-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Tsugawa Shrine (Q11597137)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Izumi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Izumi district|ja|和泉郡|zh|和泉郡|WD=Q10982247|1=Izumi district}} || {{ill|Tsugawa Shrine|lt=Tsugawa Shrine|ja|積川神社|commons|Category:Tsugawa-jinja|WD=Q11597137|1=Tsugawa Shrine}} || {{ill|Kanpei-sha|WD=Q135160338|lt=Kanpei|comment=categorized|1=Kanpei-sha}} ({{ill|Shrines receiving hoe offering|WD=Q135009205|lt=Hoe|1=Shrines receiving Hoe offering}}) || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || 6 seats, {{ill|Shi-no-Miya|WD=Q134917307|lt=Shi-no-Miya|1=Shi-no-Miya}} || — || {{coord|34.425060|135.439460|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181008 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11597137}}==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Tsugawa-jinja]] [[ja:積川神社]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kishiwada, Osaka]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Izumi Province]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Izumi Province]] [[Category:Shi-no-Miya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shrines receiving Hoe offering]] [[Category:Kanpei-sha]]
